teamObjective.explanation: Player 0 (opponent) is near finishing with only 2 cards and led a low heart 3. Taking control with the cheapest winning card stops them from freely continuing and secures the lead for our team.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 3) holds 3 cards and has not played yet, so they likely have some covering strength, but I cannot assume any specific high cards. They may be able to support me later if an opponent takes control.

opponentBelief.summary: Player 0 led a low heart 3 and has only 2 cards left, signaling they are trying to shed a weak card to get closer to going out. Their remaining card is unknown and could be low or high. Player 2 has 3 cards with no public information.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Play the heart 4: it is the lowest legal winning single, takes the lead away from the near-finish opponent, and preserves the stronger heart 9 and heart 10 for future tricks.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Passing lets player 0 win this trick and lead again with only one card left, making it easier for them to finish.
The 9 wins the trick but overcommits strength; the 4 already wins, so saving the 9 is better for later tricks.
The 10 is stronger than needed and should be held for a higher-leverage trick later.

risks: Player 0 may beat the heart 4 with their remaining card and then lead their final card to go out.
Player 2 may overtake and take control.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Using the minimal winner keeps the 9 and 10 in hand to regain or contest the lead on later tricks, and partner may cover if an opponent plays a high card.
